title: senecio santiagoensis
intro: Senecio santiagoensis, also known as Santiago ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae, native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes in South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Senecio santiagoensis include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for herbal use, and it is also used in traditional Andean medicine to treat respiratory issues and other ailments.
